findings.evidence: Method body contains 7 lines and performs multiple tasks: takeOrder, hurryUpChef, calmCustomerDown, deliverPizzaToCustomer, askForReceipt.
The takeOrder method is called twice with the same argument 'Cheese' at lines 74 and 75.
Method calls chef.cleanKitchen() at line 81, creating a chain of one method call.
The middlemanMethod at line 86 delegates to middleMethod.
Updating contact information requires changes to multiple fields (firstName, lastName, address, phoneNumber, email) at lines 101-105.
The class has multiple methods (notifyForPromotion, notifyForDiscount, notifyForNewArrivals) that would change for different reasons.
The methods applyDiscount and applyLoyaltyPoints suggest a parallel inheritance hierarchy.
The method uses an if-else chain to handle different complaints at lines 156-163.
The method refusedBequest is empty, indicating a refusal of inherited behavior.
The method accesses the private isBusy() method of the Chef class at line 173.
The boolean field frequentCustomerDiscount uses a primitive type to represent a concept that could be better represented by a small object.
The fields firstName, lastName, address, phoneNumber, and email appear together as a group.
The tempDiscountCode field is likely only used temporarily.
The tempOrderNote field is likely only used temporarily.
The askForReceipt method is unused and appears to be a speculative addition.
The anotherUnusedMethod method is never called.

Updating contact info requires changes in multiple methods.

Separate method to update email.
Methods `notifyForPromotion`, `notifyForDiscount`, and `notifyForNewArrivals` represent unrelated changes.
Methods `applyDiscount` and `applyLoyaltyPoints` suggest parallel hierarchies.
Long `if-else if` chain on `complaint`.
Method `askForReceipt` is unused.
Method `anotherUnusedMethod` is never invoked.
